questions.text: Pick the odd word out:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: All the other words signify honesty. Biased means not being honest and having favourites.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. Tanya is older than Eric. Cliff is older than Tanya. Eric is older than Cliff. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: Because the first two statements are true, Eric is the youngest of the three, so the third statement must be false.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. Blueberries cost more than strawberries. Blueberries cost less than raspberries. Raspberries cost more than both strawberries and blueberries. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: Because the first two statements are true, raspberries are the most expensive of the three.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. Mara runs faster than Gail. Lily runs faster than Mara. Gail runs faster than Lily. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: We know from the first two statements that Lily runs fastest. Therefore, the third statement must be false.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. During the past year, Josh saw more movies than Stephen. Stephen saw fewer movies than Darren. Darren saw more movies than Josh. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: Because the first two sentences are true, both Josh and Darren saw more movies than Stephen. However, it is uncertain as to whether Darren saw more movies than Josh.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. All the offices on the 9th floor have wall-to-wall carpeting. No wall-to-wall carpeting is pink. None of the offices on the 9th floor has pink wall-to-wall carpeting. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: If no wall-to-wall carpeting is pink and all the offices have wall-to-wall carpeting, none of the offices has pink wall-to-wall carpeting.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. Class A has a higher enrollment than Class B. Class C has a lower enrollment than Class B. Class A has a lower enrollment than Class C. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: From the first two statements, we know that of the three classes, Class A has the highest enrollment, so the third statement must be false.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. A fruit basket contains more apples than lemons. There are more lemons in the basket than there are oranges. The basket contains more apples than oranges. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: There are fewer oranges than either apples or lemons, so the statement is true. Easy method: (Try this method to solve without any confusion) 1. A fruit basket contains more apples than lemons = App > Lem 2. There are more lemons in the basket than there are oranges = Lem > Org Now, Combine the above two results: App > Lem > Org 3. The basket contains more apples than oranges (App > ... > Org) = Yes. Therefore, the given 3rd statement is true.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. Joe is younger than Kathy. Mark was born after Joe. Kathy is older than Mark. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: Joe is younger than Kathy and older than Mark, so Mark must be younger than Kathy.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. The temperature on Monday was lower than on Tuesday. The temperature on Wednesday was lower than on Tuesday. The temperature on Monday was higher than on Wednesday. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: We know from the first two statements that Tuesday had the highest temperature, but we cannot know whether Monday's temperature was higher than Tuesday's.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. Oat cereal has more fiber than corn cereal but less fiber than bran cereal. Corn cereal has more fiber than rice cereal but less fiber than wheat cereal. Of the three kinds of cereal, rice cereal has the least amount of fiber. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: From the first statement, we know that bran cereal has more fiber than both oat cereal and corn cereal. From the second statement, we know that rice cereal has less fiber than both corn and wheat cereals. Therefore, rice cereal has the least amount of fiber.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. Battery X lasts longer than Battery Y. Battery Y doesn't last as long as Battery Z. Battery Z lasts longer than Battery X. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: The first two statements indicate that Battery Y lasts the least amount of time, but it cannot be determined if Battery Z lasts longer than Battery X.

questions.text: Directions: Read the following information to answer.There is a family of six persons G, J, B, E, T and Z. Their professions are teacher, writer, actor, singer, dancer and lawyer but not respectively in the same order. G is a teacher. Z is a dancer. B is neither writer nor actor. E is neither singer nor lawyer. T is an actor. J is a lawyer.Which profession does E belong to?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Let's analyze the information:1. G is a teacher.2. Z is a dancer.3. B is neither writer nor actor, so B must be either singer or lawyer. But since J is a lawyer (6), B must be the singer.4. E is neither singer nor lawyer, so E must be either writer or teacher. But since G is already the teacher (1), E must be the writer.5. T is an actor (5).So, the professions are:- G: Teacher- J: Lawyer- B: Singer- E: Writer- T: Actor- Z: DancerTherefore, E belongs to the profession of Writer.

questions.text: Directions: Read the following information and give answer.Three persons A, B and C wore shirts of black, blue and orange colours (not necessarily in that order) and pants of green, yellow and orange colours (not necessarily in that order). No person wore pant and shirt of the same colour. Further, it is given that:(i) A did not wear shirt of black colour.(ii) B did not wear shirt of blue colour.(iii) C did not wear shirt of orange colour.(iv) A did not wear pant of green colour.(v) B wore pant of orange colour.What were the colours of pant and shirt worn by C, respectively?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Let's break down the information step by step:1. A did not wear a black shirt, so A must wear either blue or orange.2. B did not wear a blue shirt, so B must wear either black or orange. But since B wore orange pants (v), B must wear black shirt.3. C did not wear an orange shirt (iii), so C must wear either black or blue. But since A and B already have their shirts, C must wear blue shirt.4. A did not wear green pants (iv), so A must wear either yellow or orange. But since B already wears orange pants, A must wear yellow pants.5. Now, the only remaining combination is C wearing green pants.So, the colours of pant and shirt worn by C are:- Pant: Green- Shirt: BlueGreat problem! It's a nice example of using logical deductions to solve a puzzle.

questions.text: Directions: Read the following information and give answer.There is a family of six persons E, 1, R. K, T, and Y. I and R are sisters. T is mother of R and E is son-in-law of K. K is father of is grandson of T and I is mother.How is R is related to Y?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Let's break down the information step by step:1. I and R are sisters.2. T is the mother of R and E is the son-in-law of K, which means E is married to one of K's children.3. K is the father of T, which makes K the grandfather of R and I.4. I is the mother of Y, which makes Y the grandson of T and K.Now, let's analyze the relationships:- R is the sister of I.- I is the mother of Y.- R is the aunt of Y (since R is the sister of I, and I is the mother of Y).Therefore, R is the aunt of Y.Answer: Aunt.
Y is not the child of R, so R cannot be Y's niece.
R is a sister, but Y is not her sibling, so R cannot be Y's sister.
R is not the mother of Y, so R cannot be Y's mother.

questions.explanation: R is the sister of I, who is Y's mother. Hence, R is Y's aunt.Niece: Y is not the child of R, so R cannot be Y's niece.R is a sister, but Y is not her sibling, so R cannot be Y's sister.R is not the mother of Y, so R cannot be Y's mother..

questions.text: Directions: Read the following information and give answer.A, B. C, D and E are five friends. B is elder to E, but not as tall as C. C is younger to A. and is taller to D and E. A is taller to D, but younger than E. D is elder to A but is shortest in the group.Which of the following pairs of students is elder to D?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Let's analyze the information step by step:1. B is elder to E.2. C is younger to A, but taller than D and E.3. A is taller than D, but younger than E.4. D is elder to A, but shortest in the group.From the above information, we can deduce the following:- E is elder to B, A, C, and D (since B is elder to E, but E is not elder to B, it means E is actually younger than B is not true, but E is elder to A, C and D)- A is younger than E, but elder to C (since C is younger to A)- C is younger to A, but taller than D and E- D is elder to A, but shortest in the groupNow, let's find the pairs of students elder to D:- E is elder to D (directly mentioned)- B is elder to E, which means B is also elder to D (transitive relation)- A is younger than E, but we don't know A's relation with B directly, however A is younger than E and E is younger than B, hence B is elder to A and A is elder to D doesn't make A elder to D but B is elder to D via E and also via A indirectly- C is younger to A, and A is younger to E, and E is younger to B, hence C is younger to B, and B is elder to DTherefore, the pairs of students elder to D are:- E and BHowever, since the question asks for "pairs," the correct answer would be the combination of two individuals. Considering this, we can say:- B and EAnswer: B and E.

questions.explanation: Given Information: - A, B, C, D and E are five friends. - B is elder to E, but not as tall as C. - C is younger to A, and is taller to D and E. - A is taller to D, but younger than E. - D is elder to A but is shortest in the group. To find: Which of the following pairs of students is elder to D? Solution: Let's try to summarize the given information and create a table to better understand the relations between the friends: Friend | Age | Height --- | --- | --- A | Younger than D and taller than E | Taller than D B | Elder than E and shorter than C | --- C | Younger than A and taller than D and E | Taller than D and E D | Elder than A and shortest in the group | Shortest in the group E | Elder than A and shorter than C | --- From the table, we can see that: - B is elder to E, but not to D. - C is younger to A, and is taller to D and E, but not elder to D. - A is younger than E, but taller than D, and not elder to D. - D is elder to A, but shortest in the group, so no one is elder to D.Read more at:

questions.explanation: Given Information: - A, B, C, D and E are five friends. - B is elder to E, but not as tall as C. - C is younger to A, and is taller to D and E. - A is taller to D, but younger than E. - D is elder to A but is shortest in the group. To find: Which of the following pairs of students is elder to D? Solution: Let's try to summarize the given information and create a table to better understand the relations between the friends: Friend | Age | Height --- | --- | --- A | Younger than D and taller than E | Taller than D B | Elder than E and shorter than C | --- C | Younger than A and taller than D and E | Taller than D and E D | Elder than A and shortest in the group | Shortest in the group E | Elder than A and shorter than C | --- From the table, we can see that: - B is elder to E, but not to D. - C is younger to A, and is taller to D and E, but not elder to D. - A is younger than E, but taller than D, and not elder to D. - D is elder to A, but shortest in the group, so no one is elder to D.Read more at:

questions.text: Directions: Read the following information and give answer.There are six persons A, B, C, D, E and F. C is the sister of F. B is the brother of E's husband. D is the father of A and grandfather of F. There are two fathers, three brothers and amother in the group.Which of the following is a group of brothers?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: D is father of A and grandfather of F. So, A is father of F. Thus, D and A are the two fathers. C is the sister of F. So, C is the daughter of A. Since there is only one mother, it is evident that E is the wife of A and hence the mother of C and C and F. So, B is brother of A. There are three brothers. So, F is the brother of C.Clearly, B and A are brothers. F is the brother of C. So, three brothers are A, B, F.

questions.text: Directions: Read the following information and give answer. The post office is to the east of the school while my house is to the south of the school. The market is to the north of the post office. If the distance of the market from the post office is equal to the distance of my house from the school, in which direction is the market with respect to my school?
